isolation: Firecracker microVM (own kernel, filesystem, and network namespace)
name: AWS Lambda MicroVMs
notes: When idle, a MicroVM suspends and preserves memory and disk state while reducing costs; it resumes with state intact when traffic returns. Suspend and resume can be configured through lifecycle policies or triggered via API calls to suspend-microvm and resume-microvm.
summary: AWS Lambda MicroVMs are a serverless compute primitive within AWS Lambda that run user- or AI-generated code in isolated, stateful execution environments built on Firecracker. AWS positions them for multi-tenant applications that need to give each session its own isolated sandbox, including AI code execution. Developers call run-microvm to launch from a snapshot, connect through a dedicated HTTPS endpoint, and suspend or resume via API.
